title: Temperature Shock Utility Bill Anxiety Google Spike
thesis: Extreme heat or cold events trigger simultaneous Google Trends spikes in utility cost anxiety searches, signaling consumer stress pickup and household budget pressure. Elevated utility bill anxiety dampens discretionary spending; consumer goods and leisure stocks face demand leakage.
data_sources: Google Trends weekly 'electricity bill shock' + 'high utility costs' through google_trends adapter, paired with Open-Meteo daily max temps for regional anomaly
signal_logic: If weekly Trends index for utility bill keywords >65 AND regional temp anomaly >+8°F or <-8°F from 30-year normal, short XLY
entry_exit: Entry: If weekly Trends index for utility bill keywords >65 AND regional temp anomaly >+8°F or <-8°F from 30-year normal, short XLY Exit: After 7 trading days or when Trends index falls below 50
position_sizing: Allocate 5% per signal, max 20% total exposure.
risks: Data source instability, false positives, and regime shifts.
implementation_notes: Use Google Trends weekly 'electricity bill shock' + 'high utility costs' through google_trends adapter, paired with Open-Meteo daily max temps for regional anomaly via api (weekly).
reason_it_will_fire_in_30_days: Seasonal temperature extremes (summer heat, winter cold) occur reliably; anxiety spikes fire monthly in most years.

schema.idea: Temperature Shock Utility Bill Anxiety Google Spike

data.source: Google Trends weekly 'electricity bill shock' + 'high utility costs' through google_trends adapter, paired with Open-Meteo daily max temps for regional anomaly

schema.behavior: Extreme heat or cold events trigger simultaneous Google Trends spikes in utility cost anxiety searches, signaling consumer stress pickup and household budget pressure.

market_impact.rationale: Elevated utility bill anxiety dampens discretionary spending; consumer goods and leisure stocks face demand leakage.

trade_logic.entry: If weekly Trends index for utility bill keywords >65 AND regional temp anomaly >+8°F or <-8°F from 30-year normal, short XLY
trade_logic.exit: After 7 trading days or when Trends index falls below 50

schema.reason_it_will_fire_in_30_days: Seasonal temperature extremes (summer heat, winter cold) occur reliably; anxiety spikes fire monthly in most years.

title: News Rss Surge On Semiconductor Supply Bottleneck
thesis: Spikes in news article volume about semiconductor supply constraints correlate with short-term panic selling in tech and industrial sectors, creating oversold rebound opportunities. Extreme attention to chip shortages often precedes capitulation and technical rebound in semiconductor-exposed tech.
data_sources: RSS feed article count (via rss_count adapter) filtering for 'semiconductor shortage' + 'chip supply' from major tech/finance outlets, daily
signal_logic: If daily RSS article count on semiconductor bottleneck exceeds 50 articles and is 2 standard deviations above 30-day mean
entry_exit: Entry: If daily RSS article count on semiconductor bottleneck exceeds 50 articles and is 2 standard deviations above 30-day mean Exit: After 6 trading days or if article count falls below 20
position_sizing: Allocate 5% per signal, max 20% total exposure.
risks: Data source instability, false positives, and regime shifts.
implementation_notes: Use RSS feed article count (via rss_count adapter) filtering for 'semiconductor shortage' + 'chip supply' from major tech/finance outlets, daily via scrape (daily).
reason_it_will_fire_in_30_days: Supply chain disruption narratives resurge monthly; 50+ articles in a day fires multiple times per quarter.

schema.idea: News RSS Surge on Semiconductor Supply Bottleneck

data.source: RSS feed article count (via rss_count adapter) filtering for 'semiconductor shortage' + 'chip supply' from major tech/finance outlets, daily

schema.behavior: Spikes in news article volume about semiconductor supply constraints correlate with short-term panic selling in tech and industrial sectors, creating oversold rebound opportunities.

market_impact.rationale: Extreme attention to chip shortages often precedes capitulation and technical rebound in semiconductor-exposed tech.

trade_logic.entry: If daily RSS article count on semiconductor bottleneck exceeds 50 articles and is 2 standard deviations above 30-day mean
trade_logic.exit: After 6 trading days or if article count falls below 20

schema.reason_it_will_fire_in_30_days: Supply chain disruption narratives resurge monthly; 50+ articles in a day fires multiple times per quarter.

title: Google Trends Surge In Flight Delay Complaints
thesis: Sharp spikes in Google search volume for flight delay complaints signal operational stress in airlines and airports, often preceding earnings pressure or guidance cuts. Consumer discretionary travel sentiment deteriorates; airlines and leisure stocks face margin and demand headwinds.
data_sources: Google Trends weekly search interest for 'flight delays' + 'airline cancellation' through google_trends adapter
signal_logic: If weekly Google Trends index for 'flight delays' exceeds 70 (out of 100) and rises 15+ points from prior week
entry_exit: Entry: If weekly Google Trends index for 'flight delays' exceeds 70 (out of 100) and rises 15+ points from prior week Exit: After 10 trading days or when index falls below 55
position_sizing: Allocate 5% per signal, max 20% total exposure.
risks: Data source instability, false positives, and regime shifts.
implementation_notes: Use Google Trends weekly search interest for 'flight delays' + 'airline cancellation' through google_trends adapter via api (weekly).
reason_it_will_fire_in_30_days: Seasonal airline disruptions, weather events, and staffing crises drive this signal weekly in peak travel seasons; fires at least monthly year-round.

schema.idea: Google Trends Surge in Flight Delay Complaints

data.source: Google Trends weekly search interest for 'flight delays' + 'airline cancellation' through google_trends adapter

schema.behavior: Sharp spikes in Google search volume for flight delay complaints signal operational stress in airlines and airports, often preceding earnings pressure or guidance cuts.

market_impact.rationale: Consumer discretionary travel sentiment deteriorates; airlines and leisure stocks face margin and demand headwinds.

trade_logic.entry: If weekly Google Trends index for 'flight delays' exceeds 70 (out of 100) and rises 15+ points from prior week
trade_logic.exit: After 10 trading days or when index falls below 55

schema.reason_it_will_fire_in_30_days: Seasonal airline disruptions, weather events, and staffing crises drive this signal weekly in peak travel seasons; fires at least monthly year-round.

title: Earthquake Activity Reinsurance Volatility Spike
thesis: Clustering of moderate-to-strong earthquakes in populated zones triggers rapid repricing of catastrophe reinsurance and property-linked derivatives, creating short-term financial sector rotation. Insurance and reinsurance stocks face claim reserve repricing; financial sector sentiment shifts on tail-risk realization.
data_sources: USGS earthquake API magnitude 4.5+ events by week through earthquake_activity adapter
signal_logic: If 3+ magnitude 4.5+ earthquakes occur within a 7-day rolling window in the continental US or California, short XLF
entry_exit: Entry: If 3+ magnitude 4.5+ earthquakes occur within a 7-day rolling window in the continental US or California, short XLF Exit: After 7 trading days or when weekly earthquake count drops to 0
position_sizing: Allocate 5% per signal, max 20% total exposure.
risks: Data source instability, false positives, and regime shifts.
implementation_notes: Use USGS earthquake API magnitude 4.5+ events by week through earthquake_activity adapter via api (weekly).
reason_it_will_fire_in_30_days: California and Western US experience earthquake clusters regularly; 3+ events in a week occurs 2–4 times per year.

data.source: USGS earthquake API magnitude 4.5+ events by week through earthquake_activity adapter

schema.behavior: Clustering of moderate-to-strong earthquakes in populated zones triggers rapid repricing of catastrophe reinsurance and property-linked derivatives, creating short-term financial sector rotation.

market_impact.rationale: Insurance and reinsurance stocks face claim reserve repricing; financial sector sentiment shifts on tail-risk realization.

trade_logic.entry: If 3+ magnitude 4.5+ earthquakes occur within a 7-day rolling window in the continental US or California, short XLF
trade_logic.exit: After 7 trading days or when weekly earthquake count drops to 0

schema.reason_it_will_fire_in_30_days: California and Western US experience earthquake clusters regularly; 3+ events in a week occurs 2–4 times per year.

title: Bts Airline Load Factor Capacity Stress Indicator
thesis: When airline load factors exceed 88% and rise >2 percentage points week-over-week, it signals capacity stress and pricing power, supporting airline stock but stressing seat availability and customer satisfaction. High load factors indicate pricing strength and full planes; airlines benefit from margin expansion.
data_sources: BTS (Bureau of Transportation Statistics) airline load factor (passenger utilization %) weekly through bts_airline_load_factor adapter
signal_logic: If weekly load factor >88% AND increases >2 pts from prior week, long XLY
entry_exit: Entry: If weekly load factor >88% AND increases >2 pts from prior week, long XLY Exit: After 8 trading days or if load factor falls below 86%
position_sizing: Allocate 5% per signal, max 20% total exposure.
risks: Data source instability, false positives, and regime shifts.
implementation_notes: Use BTS (Bureau of Transportation Statistics) airline load factor (passenger utilization %) weekly through bts_airline_load_factor adapter via api (weekly).
reason_it_will_fire_in_30_days: Peak travel seasons and capacity constraints drive load factors >88% regularly; fires 4–6 times annually.

schema.idea: BTS Airline Load Factor Capacity Stress Indicator

data.source: BTS (Bureau of Transportation Statistics) airline load factor (passenger utilization %) weekly through bts_airline_load_factor adapter

schema.behavior: When airline load factors exceed 88% and rise >2 percentage points week-over-week, it signals capacity stress and pricing power, supporting airline stock but stressing seat availability and customer satisfaction.

market_impact.rationale: High load factors indicate pricing strength and full planes; airlines benefit from margin expansion.

trade_logic.entry: If weekly load factor >88% AND increases >2 pts from prior week, long XLY
trade_logic.exit: After 8 trading days or if load factor falls below 86%

schema.reason_it_will_fire_in_30_days: Peak travel seasons and capacity constraints drive load factors >88% regularly; fires 4–6 times annually.

title: Price Momentum Reversion In Defensive Sectors After Tech Crash
thesis: When XLK underperforms XLV by >4% over 5 consecutive trading days, it often signals fear-driven rotation into defensive healthcare; subsequent mean reversion favors tech outperformance. Extreme defensive rotations are often overdone; tech rebounds as rotation exhausts.
data_sources: Yahoo Finance daily closing prices for XLK (tech) and XLV (healthcare) through price_only adapter, computing 5-day rolling returns
signal_logic: If 5-day cumulative return for XLK < XLV by >4%, long XLK relative to XLV
entry_exit: Entry: If 5-day cumulative return for XLK < XLV by >4%, long XLK relative to XLV Exit: After 6 trading days or if relative performance reverses >2%
position_sizing: Allocate 5% per signal, max 20% total exposure.
risks: Data source instability, false positives, and regime shifts.
implementation_notes: Use Yahoo Finance daily closing prices for XLK (tech) and XLV (healthcare) through price_only adapter, computing 5-day rolling returns via api (daily).
reason_it_will_fire_in_30_days: Daily volatility and sector rotation create 4%+ relative moves weekly; signal fires multiple times per month.

schema.idea: Price Momentum Reversion in Defensive Sectors After Tech Crash

data.source: Yahoo Finance daily closing prices for XLK (tech) and XLV (healthcare) through price_only adapter, computing 5-day rolling returns

schema.behavior: When XLK underperforms XLV by >4% over 5 consecutive trading days, it often signals fear-driven rotation into defensive healthcare; subsequent mean reversion favors tech outperformance.

market_impact.rationale: Extreme defensive rotations are often overdone; tech rebounds as rotation exhausts.

trade_logic.entry: If 5-day cumulative return for XLK < XLV by >4%, long XLK relative to XLV
trade_logic.exit: After 6 trading days or if relative performance reverses >2%

schema.reason_it_will_fire_in_30_days: Daily volatility and sector rotation create 4%+ relative moves weekly; signal fires multiple times per month.

title: Ev Charger Network Expansion Velocity Surge
thesis: Rapid week-over-week increases in public EV charger installations signal acceleration in green infrastructure capex, indicating bullish macro stimulus or corporate EV investment push. Industrial sector benefits from infrastructure spending and EV supply chain acceleration.
data_sources: OpenChargeMap API daily charger count by region through openchargemap adapter
signal_logic: If weekly charger count increase exceeds 3% week-over-week and 7-day moving average is positive
entry_exit: Entry: If weekly charger count increase exceeds 3% week-over-week and 7-day moving average is positive Exit: After 10 trading days or if weekly growth falls below 1%
position_sizing: Allocate 5% per signal, max 20% total exposure.
risks: Data source instability, false positives, and regime shifts.
implementation_notes: Use OpenChargeMap API daily charger count by region through openchargemap adapter via api (daily).
reason_it_will_fire_in_30_days: EV charger networks expand consistently; 3% weekly growth occurs multiple times per quarter in most regions.

data.source: OpenChargeMap API daily charger count by region through openchargemap adapter

market_impact.rationale: Industrial sector benefits from infrastructure spending and EV supply chain acceleration.

trade_logic.entry: If weekly charger count increase exceeds 3% week-over-week and 7-day moving average is positive
trade_logic.exit: After 10 trading days or if weekly growth falls below 1%

schema.reason_it_will_fire_in_30_days: EV charger networks expand consistently; 3% weekly growth occurs multiple times per quarter in most regions.

title: Fred Jobless Claims Volatility Pop Reversion
thesis: When initial jobless claims spike 20% week-over-week (often noise-driven or seasonal), they often revert sharply the following week, creating mean-reversion trading opportunity in defensive and cyclical sectors. Extreme jobless claim spikes are often false signals or seasonal; mean reversion favors cyclical consumer stocks.
data_sources: FRED series ICSA (Initial Jobless Claims) weekly through fred_series adapter
signal_logic: If weekly ICSA increases >20% from prior week, enter long XLY at close
entry_exit: Entry: If weekly ICSA increases >20% from prior week, enter long XLY at close Exit: After 5 trading days or if ICSA falls week-over-week
position_sizing: Allocate 5% per signal, max 20% total exposure.
risks: Data source instability, false positives, and regime shifts.
implementation_notes: Use FRED series ICSA (Initial Jobless Claims) weekly through fred_series adapter via api (weekly).
reason_it_will_fire_in_30_days: Seasonal and holiday-related jobless claim spikes occur predictably; >20% moves happen 3–5 times annually.

schema.idea: FRED Jobless Claims Volatility Pop Reversion

data.source: FRED series ICSA (Initial Jobless Claims) weekly through fred_series adapter

schema.behavior: When initial jobless claims spike 20% week-over-week (often noise-driven or seasonal), they often revert sharply the following week, creating mean-reversion trading opportunity in defensive and cyclical sectors.

market_impact.rationale: Extreme jobless claim spikes are often false signals or seasonal; mean reversion favors cyclical consumer stocks.

trade_logic.entry: If weekly ICSA increases >20% from prior week, enter long XLY at close
trade_logic.exit: After 5 trading days or if ICSA falls week-over-week

schema.reason_it_will_fire_in_30_days: Seasonal and holiday-related jobless claim spikes occur predictably; >20% moves happen 3–5 times annually.

title: Eia Electricity Demand Shock Rebound Spread
thesis: Sharp week-over-week drops in electricity demand (>8%) often indicate demand shock or recession fears, which revert quickly as underlying activity resumes or weather normalizes. Demand collapses are often temporary; utilities rebound as demand normalizes, and utility stocks offer defensive stability.
data_sources: EIA weekly electricity demand (net generation MWh) through eia_electricity adapter, tracked regionally
signal_logic: If weekly electricity demand drops >8% from prior week, long XLU
entry_exit: Entry: If weekly electricity demand drops >8% from prior week, long XLU Exit: After 7 trading days or if demand recovers >4%
position_sizing: Allocate 5% per signal, max 20% total exposure.
risks: Data source instability, false positives, and regime shifts.
implementation_notes: Use EIA weekly electricity demand (net generation MWh) through eia_electricity adapter, tracked regionally via api (weekly).
reason_it_will_fire_in_30_days: Seasonal demand swings, weather volatility, and demand shocks produce >8% weekly moves 3–5 times annually.

schema.idea: EIA Electricity Demand Shock Rebound Spread

data.source: EIA weekly electricity demand (net generation MWh) through eia_electricity adapter, tracked regionally

schema.behavior: Sharp week-over-week drops in electricity demand (>8%) often indicate demand shock or recession fears, which revert quickly as underlying activity resumes or weather normalizes.

market_impact.rationale: Demand collapses are often temporary; utilities rebound as demand normalizes, and utility stocks offer defensive stability.

trade_logic.entry: If weekly electricity demand drops >8% from prior week, long XLU
trade_logic.exit: After 7 trading days or if demand recovers >4%

schema.reason_it_will_fire_in_30_days: Seasonal demand swings, weather volatility, and demand shocks produce >8% weekly moves 3–5 times annually.

title: Port Container Velocity Slowdown Liquidation
thesis: When weekly port container volumes drop >15% from the prior week (signaling demand shock or inventory flush), logistics and industrial stocks face sharp repricing. Container volume collapse signals upstream demand destruction, freight rate pressure, and logistics capex cuts.
data_sources: Port container volume (TEU) via port_container_volume adapter for top 5 US ports, weekly
signal_logic: If aggregate 5-port TEU volume drops >15% week-over-week, short XLI
entry_exit: Entry: If aggregate 5-port TEU volume drops >15% week-over-week, short XLI Exit: After 8 trading days or when weekly volume rebounds >5%
position_sizing: Allocate 5% per signal, max 20% total exposure.
risks: Data source instability, false positives, and regime shifts.
implementation_notes: Use Port container volume (TEU) via port_container_volume adapter for top 5 US ports, weekly via api (weekly).
reason_it_will_fire_in_30_days: Post-holiday liquidation, seasonal demand shifts, and supply-chain corrections produce >15% weekly swings 2–4 times annually.

data.source: Port container volume (TEU) via port_container_volume adapter for top 5 US ports, weekly

schema.behavior: When weekly port container volumes drop >15% from the prior week (signaling demand shock or inventory flush), logistics and industrial stocks face sharp repricing.

market_impact.rationale: Container volume collapse signals upstream demand destruction, freight rate pressure, and logistics capex cuts.

trade_logic.entry: If aggregate 5-port TEU volume drops >15% week-over-week, short XLI
trade_logic.exit: After 8 trading days or when weekly volume rebounds >5%

schema.reason_it_will_fire_in_30_days: Post-holiday liquidation, seasonal demand shifts, and supply-chain corrections produce >15% weekly swings 2–4 times annually.
